title: Sweet Fluffy Kenyan Lamb Stew
description: A onepot, ketofriendly stew featuring cured lamb shoulder and subtle sweetness that turns into a fluffy, aromatic dish.
instructions: Rinse the cured lamb shoulder and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a Dutch oven, heat coconut oil over medium‑high heat. Brown the lamb on all sides, about 5‑6 minutes, then transfer to a plate.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger to the pot and cook until the onion becomes translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika, cumin, and coriander; cook for 1 minute to release the spices’ aromas.
Return the browned lamb to the pot, pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk, and stir to combine.
Add the monk fruit sweetener and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the stew has thickened to a fluffy consistency.
Remove the lid, stir in fresh lemon juice to brighten the flavors, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired.
Enjoy the sweet, airy, and hearty Kenyan lamb stew, perfectly suited for a keto dinner.
